seoTitle: How to Design Magazine Inner Pages in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to design magazine inner content pages in Canva, from choosing layouts to arranging text and images for a professional finish.
intro: Beyond the cover, a magazine's inner pages need to balance text and imagery in a way that's easy to read and visually engaging. Canva's multi-page templates make this achievable without dedicated publishing software, which is helpful for freelancers, students, and small publishers. This guide covers building out an inner spread.

steps.heading: Choose an inner-page layout template
steps.body: Search Canva's template library for magazine or editorial layouts designed for body content rather than covers.

steps.heading: Set up your text columns
steps.body: Add or adjust text boxes to create a multi-column layout that's easier to read at magazine size.

steps.heading: Place supporting images
steps.body: Drop in photos or graphics that support the article, resizing them to fit cleanly within the column grid.

steps.heading: Refine typography for readability
steps.body: Choose consistent heading and body fonts, keeping sizes appropriate for print or digital reading.

steps.heading: Export your finished spread
steps.body: Download the pages as a PDF, keeping page order consistent if it's part of a larger magazine.

faq.question: Can I keep consistent styling across multiple pages?
faq.answer: Yes, you can copy text and image styles between pages, or build a template page to duplicate for consistency.

faq.question: Does Canva support multi-column text layouts?
faq.answer: Yes, either through templates built with columns already, or by manually placing separate text boxes side by side.

faq.question: What resolution should images be for print?
faq.answer: Aim for high-resolution images (300 DPI equivalent) so they stay sharp when printed at magazine size.

faq.question: Can I collaborate with others on the design?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva supports real-time collaboration, so multiple people can work on the same magazine project.
